channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: High‑profile privacy case against the Daily Mail: key moments in the hacking trial
item.description: The article recounts the final stages of a major High Court trial in which Prince Harry, Elton John, David Furnish, actors Sadie Frost and Elizabeth Hurley, campaigner Doreen Lawrence and former polit...

item.title: Middle East conflict causing global shipping and fuel cost surge
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, is severely impacting global shipping costs. Ships are hesitant to sail through the Gulf due to safety co...

item.title: Senate orders Police to investigate deadly Jos attacks
item.description: The Nigerian Senate don strongly condemn the brutal attack wey happen for Rukuba village, Jos North LGA, Plateau State, wey scatter innocent people and cause heavy casualties. Dem don direct the Inspe...

item.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina defeat Italy in World Cup playoff, ending Italy's qualification streak
item.description: Italy’s hopes of returning to the FIFA World Cup suffered another blow as they were defeated by Bosnia and Herzegovina in a dramatic playoff final. After a 1-1 draw in regulation time, the match went ...

item.title: Overview of Global Social Media Restrictions for Minors
item.description: Since Australia introduced a ban on social media for children under 16 in December 2025, other countries have begun considering or implementing similar measures to protect minors online. Australia’s l...

item.title: Onzieme restaurant in Kingston prepares for its final service
item.description: Onzieme, a cherished restaurant in Kingston, is preparing to close its doors following the recent announcement by owner and chef Louis Couttoupes. Known for its dynamic and inventive approach to dinin...

item.title: Bafana’s World Cup preparation hit as Panama edge friendly in Cape Town
item.description: Bafana Bafana suffered a 2-1 home defeat against Panama in a friendly at Cape Town Stadium on Tuesday night, their first loss at home under coach Hugo Broos in five years. Despite flashes of promise, ...

item.title: Poilievre Criticizes Toronto–Quebec City High-Speed Rail Plan as Costly and Unnecessary
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre has called on the federal government to cancel its proposed high-speed rail project that would connect Toronto and Quebec City. During a news conference near Peter...

item.title: LNG Supply Disruption Affects Power Generation Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Government officials in Pakistan revealed during a Nepra public hearing that LNG supplies are currently under force majeure and cannot be used for power generation. This disruption is due to the ongoi...

item.title: Child prosecuted under new UK law after piloting small boat across the Channel
item.description: A child has been charged in the United Kingdom with endangering others during a small boat crossing of the English Channel, marking one of the first prosecutions under a new and controversial immigrat...

item.title: Supergirl Trailer Released Starring Milly Alcock and Jason Momoa, Hits Cinemas June 26
item.description: The official trailer for 'Supergirl', directed by Craig Gillespie, has been released, generating excitement among superhero film fans. The movie features Milly Alcock taking on the role of Supergirl, ...

item.title: NHL introduces real-time playoff salary cap tool for team compliance
item.description: The National Hockey League (NHL) has introduced a new policy requiring teams to stay under a playoff salary cap, marking the end of a long-standing loophole where clubs could exceed the cap by using p...

item.title: LTFRB Approves 10% Fare Discount for Yanson Group Buses During Holy Week 2026
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has approved a 10% fare discount for passengers riding buses operated by the Yanson Group of Bus Companies from Good Friday to Easter S...

item.title: Treasury Confirms Timely Payment of Sh53.56bn Bond Interest, Clears Arrears Confusion
item.description: The National Treasury of Kenya has clarified concerns around Treasury bond interest payments worth Sh53.56 billion for May and June 2025. The Treasury stated that all payments were made on time and fu...

item.title: Metrobank Launches H.A.N.D.S. to Help Filipinos Manage Finances Amid Uncertain Times
item.description: Metrobank has introduced H.A.N.D.S., a practical financial guide designed to help Filipinos manage their money more effectively, especially during uncertain times. H.A.N.D.S. focuses on five key actio...

item.title: Legal Review of Nyamira Hospital in Kericho Mass Grave Investigation
item.description: Investigations into the mass grave discovered in Kericho have shifted focus from murder to possible criminal negligence, mishandling of human remains, and failures in documentation. Detectives are now...

item.title: UCP MLA Advocates for Alberta Independence Referendum, Premier Smith Maintains Neutral Stance
item.description: Recent developments within Premier Danielle Smith’s United Conservative Party (UCP) highlight a growing separatist sentiment among some members. Jason Stephan, MLA for Red Deer-South and parliamentary...

item.title: E.M Hospital launches modern CT scan and dialysis services to serve locals
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Deputy Minister for Health, Dr Florence Samizi, has praised E.M Hospital in Kigamboni for making major investments in health technology. The hospital recently launched a modern CT ...

item.title: After transgender rights bill amendment, confusion over validity of 32,000 self‑identified TG certificates
item.description: After President Droupadi Murmu gave assent to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, transgender community members across India are worried about the fate of over 32,000 ...

item.title: Ukraine Targets Ust-Luga Port in Multiple Drone Strikes, Disrupting Oil Exports
item.description: On Tuesday, Ukrainian drones attacked Russia's Ust-Luga port on the Baltic Sea for the fifth time in just ten days, targeting the oil loading facilities managed by Transneft. This series of strikes fo...
